title: BehaveSense: Continuous authentication for security-sensitive mobile apps using behavioral biometrics

method.input_representation: Feature vectors (coordinates, pressure, velocity, duration) extracted from four types of touch operations (clicking, vertical sliding, horizontal sliding, oblique sliding)
method.training_strategy: Anomaly detection based on the owner's behavioral patterns to distinguish owner from unknown impostors.

evaluation_protocol: Leave-one-out subject-wise validation; owner model trained on a subset of samples and tested against remaining owner samples and samples from all other participants acting as impostors.

reported_limitations: Difficulty in collecting enough samples for infrequent touch operations (e.g., zoom)

key_contribution: Proposed BehaveSense, a continuous authentication system using one-class anomaly detection (OCSVM) on touch sequences to avoid the need for impostor training data.
Implemented a Bayesian-based confidence level calculation and an improved expectedprob algorithm for sequence authentication.
main_strength: Evaluated on a real-world dataset of users interacting with a popular app (WeChat) over two weeks.
Extremely low energy overhead and computational latency.
main_weakness: Lack of active spoofing/presentation attack testing.
Use of private data and no provided code inhibits reproducibility.
insights.notes_for_review: The paper reports high accuracy (95.85%), but does not explicitly report the target metrics (AUC, EER, FAR, FRR) in the text or tables using standard terminology, despite providing confusion matrices.
